functional_requirements.title: Add Expense
functional_requirements.description: User can add an expense record with amount, category, description, and date. Each record gets a unique ID. Amount must be positive. Date defaults to today. Categories: food, transport, entertainment, shopping, other.

functional_requirements.title: List Expenses
functional_requirements.description: User can list all expense records with optional filtering by category and date range (start_date, end_date). Output includes ID, amount, category, description, date.

functional_requirements.title: Update Expense
functional_requirements.description: User can update an existing expense record by ID. Fields that can be updated: amount, category, description. Check that the record exists.

functional_requirements.title: Delete Expense
functional_requirements.description: User can delete an expense record by ID. Check that the record exists.

functional_requirements.title: Get Summary
functional_requirements.description: User can get expense summary statistics grouped by category or date range. Returns total amount and amounts per category.

non_functional_requirements.description: All operations are performed via a single function call track_expense(command, expense_data).
Error handling for invalid ID, negative amount, invalid category.

acceptance_criteria.description: All 5 operations (add, list, update, delete, summary) execute correctly.
Filtering by category and date range works correctly.
Error handling for invalid ID, negative amount, invalid category.

storage.details: No persistence; data stored in memory during session.

glossary.expense record: A single entry containing amount, category, description, date, and unique ID.
glossary.category: One of food, transport, entertainment, shopping, other.
glossary.summary: Aggregated statistics of expenses, optionally grouped by category or date range.

global_state_sources.description: Counter for generating unique expense IDs.

input_spec.description: Input is provided via the track_expense function with a command string and a JSON object expense_data.

output_spec.description: Output is a JSON object with success flag, message, and optional data.
